2018-06-25.abstract: Syrian officials say there wasn’t a chemical attack. But what really took place in the town of Douma in April? We analyzed 67 videos to reconstruct how at least 34 people died in one apartment building.

2018-06-25.title: After John Oliver’s Jokes About Xi Jinping, China Blocks HBO Website
2018-06-25.abstract: A week after Mr. Oliver mocked China’s president, the authorities there have taken additional strides to block him on the internet.

2018-06-26.title: China’s Huawei Leads as Corporate Sponsor of Australian Politicians’ Travel
2018-06-26.abstract: Huawei, which could be barred from doing business in Australia over spying concerns, is the biggest corporate sponsor of overseas travel for Australia’s politicians.

2018-06-26.title: ‘Who Is This Stupid God?’ For His Latest Insult, Duterte Aims High
2018-06-26.abstract: Disparaging remarks about God by President Rodrigo Duterte have led to his sharpest clash yet with the Philippines’ politically powerful Catholic Church.

2018-06-26.title: Amid Tensions, Mattis Arrives in China to ‘Have a Conversation’
2018-06-26.abstract: Defense Secretary Jim Mattis’s visit, the first by a Pentagon chief in years, comes weeks after he accused Beijing of “intimidation” in the South China Sea.

2018-06-26.title: A Botched Statue Restoration in Spain: Is That St. George or Tintin?
2018-06-26.abstract: A church in the town of Estella asked a local workshop to give a 16th-century work a makeover. The results horrified the authorities, scandalized restorers and set social media ablaze.

2018-06-26.title: In a Chemical Weapons Debate, Russia Tries to Change the Subject
2018-06-26.abstract: What was supposed to be a debate on assigning blame for attacks turned into a discussion of what constituted a quorum and who had paid their dues.

2018-06-27.title: Tiaras, Purses and Cash: Malaysia Seizes Record Haul From Ex-Leader Najib
2018-06-27.abstract: As much as $273 million in jewelry, purses and cash was seized from former Prime Minister Najib Razak and his wife, who are embroiled in a vast graft scandal.

2018-06-27.title: The Poetry of Urban Angst
2018-06-27.abstract: Lamenting the trappings of the modern world — from our false sense of connectedness to avo toast.

2018-06-27.title: How to Survive When Money Is Worthless
2018-06-27.abstract: A year and a half after I was kicked out of Venezuela, I returned to find out how Venezuelans are holding on as the country falls apart and inflation makes buying a single cup of coffee nearly impossible.

2018-06-27.title: In Hong Kong, Where Guns Are Rare, Deadly Shooting Shocks Residents
2018-06-27.abstract: Gun crimes are exceptionally rare in the city, and the shooting of four people, two fatally, has dominated front pages and news broadcasts.

2018-06-27.title: As Search for Thai Boys Lost in Cave Hits Day 5, a Nation Holds Its Breath
2018-06-27.abstract: A dozen boys and their soccer coach, trapped in a flooded cave complex since Saturday, are the focus of a huge rescue operation and the hopes of a watching country.

2018-06-28.title: Dutch Lawmakers Approve Partial Ban on Burqas and Niqabs
2018-06-28.abstract: The law, approved by the Dutch parliament’s Upper House on Tuesday, also bans ski masks and helmets, but it would directly affect some Muslim women.

2018-06-28.title: Migration to Europe Is Down Sharply. So Is It Still a ‘Crisis’?
2018-06-28.abstract: Despite the claims of far-right leaders, the number of undocumented migrants arriving each year is back to pre-crisis levels — and has been for some time.

2018-06-28.title: As Cameroon English Speakers Fight to Break Away, Violence Mounts
2018-06-28.abstract: Some of the Central African country’s Anglophones want to found a country called Ambazonia. The French-speaking government is deeply opposed.

2018-06-28.title: Shanghai Knife Attack Kills Two Schoolchildren
2018-06-28.abstract: Mass shootings are rare in China, but children are sometimes targeted in knife attacks on schools.

2018-06-28.title: Mexican Soccer Fans’ New Champions: The South Korean Squad
2018-06-28.abstract: Germany’s World Cup loss meant Mexico would live to fight another day — and fans had South Korea to thank.

2018-06-29.title: Shadowy Cell in France Plotted to Kill Muslim Civilians, Authorities Say
2018-06-29.abstract: Ten members of a group that said it was “fighting the Islamic peril” face terrorism charges. Guns, ammunition and explosive material were seized.

2018-06-29.title: U.N. Cases Read Like ‘Manual in How Not to Investigate’ Sexual Assault
2018-06-29.abstract: The secretary general has vowed greater accountability for sexual misconduct. But many employees say that won’t end decades of sexism and abuse within the organization.

2018-06-29.title: From Jewish Saint, to Israeli Politician, to Diaspora’s Ally
2018-06-29.abstract: At 70, Natan Sharansky is reflecting on his life and career. “You never finish anything,” he says. “You do your part, and then you let others continue.”

2018-06-29.title: Thailand Cave Rescue Mission Will Continue, Prime Minister Tells Families
2018-06-29.abstract: The search for 12 boys and their soccer coach trapped behind rising floodwaters has become a national focus, with the king following events closely.

2018-06-29.title: 1 Neighborhood. 24 Kindergarten Classes. 40 Languages. (Some Miming Helps.)
2018-06-29.abstract: There are so many children in one Toronto neighborhood that the school board built an entire school just for kindergartners — 24 classes.

2018-06-30.title: A Place by a Lake or in the Woods: the Canada Letter
2018-06-30.abstract: In Canada, there’s lots of land but not many people, which has made it possible for summer “cottages” to be a part of many Canadians’ lives. Rising prices, however, may threaten that.

2018-06-30.title: More Americans Evacuated From China Over Mysterious Ailments
2018-06-30.abstract: At least 11 Americans have been moved out of the country after “abnormal” sounds were reported by consulate employees in Guangzhou.

2018-06-30.title: Spain’s Migrant Wave Grows, Even as Europe’s Subsides
2018-06-30.abstract: The new Socialist government in Madrid has waded straight into Europe’s migration crisis, winning praise but facing pressure over a growing influx.

2018-06-30.title: What It Costs to Be Smuggled Across the U.S. Border
2018-06-30.abstract: Bribes and shakedowns. Days in hideaways without food. For many fleeing violence in Central America, this is what thousands of dollars gets them on the journey to the United States.

2018-06-30.title: War Robbed Him of His Family, Then His Eyes, Then His Love
2018-06-30.abstract: Today, he marches for peace across Afghanistan. But his thoughts and poetry are fixed on the girl he lost, and bittersweet images of another life.
